name: python3-websockets
title: python3-websockets
description:
implementation of the WebSocket Protocol (RFC 6455)
websockets is a library for developing WebSocket servers and clients in
Python. It implements RFC 6455 with a focus on correctness and simplicity.
It passes the Autobahn Testsuite.
.
Built on top of Python's asynchronous I/O support introduced in PEP 3156,
it provides an API based on coroutines, making it easy to write highly
concurrent applications.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use websockets because implementation of the WebSocket Protocol (RFC 6455).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use websockets as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `websockets [options] [args]` as documented upstream for websockets.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair websockets with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `websockets --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
